entries.definition:
كتاب العين

عقو - عقب

(عقب) العين والقاف والباء أصلانِ صحيحان: أحدُهما يدلُّ على تأخير شيء وإتيانِه بعد غيره. والأصل الآخَر يدلُّ على ارتفاعٍ وشدّة وصُعوبة.

فالأوّل قال الخليل: كلُّ شيء يَعقُبُ شيئاً فهو عَقيبُه، كقولك خَلفٍ يَخلف، بمنزلة اللَّيل والنهار إذا مضى أحدُهما عَقَبَ الآخَر. وهما عَقيبانِ، كلُّ

عَفْوٌ ʿafw [v. n./n.] surplus, spare (2:219) وَيَسْأَلُونَكَ مَاذَا يُنْفِقُونَ قُلِ الْعَفْوَ
and they ask you what they should give: say, [‘Give of] the
spare’.

عَفُوٌّ ʿafuww [intens. act. part./n.] one who is disposed to
forgiving others (58:2) وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَعَفُوٌّ غَفُورٌ but God is pardoning and
forgiving.

عَافِينَ ʿāfīn [pl. of act. part. عَافٍ ʿāfī] one who forgives (3:134)
وَالْعَافِينَ عَنِ النَّاسِ and those who pardon [the offences of] people.
entries.definition_english:
عَفْوٌ ʿafw [v. n./n.] surplus, spare (2:219) وَيَسْأَلُونَكَ مَاذَا يُنْفِقُونَ قُلِ الْعَفْوَ
and they ask you what they should give: say, [‘Give of] the
spare’.

عَفُوٌّ ʿafuww [intens. act. part./n.] one who is disposed to
forgiving others (58:2) وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَعَفُوٌّ غَفُورٌ but God is pardoning and
forgiving.

عَافِينَ ʿāfīn [pl. of act. part. عَافٍ ʿāfī] one who forgives (3:134)
وَالْعَافِينَ عَنِ النَّاسِ and those who pardon [the offences of] people.

entries.definition:
ʿ–q–b heels, to arrive at the heel of, to follow, to pursue, to
investigate; eagle; to turn back; to repair; result; to avenge
oneself; to punish; to interlace; obstacle; to detain. Of this root,
14 forms occur 80 times in the Qur’an: يُعَقِّب yuʿaqqib twice; عَاقَبَ
ʿāqaba four times; عُوقِبَ ʿūqiba twice; أَعْقَبَ ʾaʿqaba once; عُقْب
ʿuqb once; عَقِب ʿaqib once; عَقِبَيْهِ ʿaqibayh three times; أَعْقَاب
ʾaʿqāb four times; عِقَاب ʿiqāb 20 times; عَقَبَةٌ ʿaqabatun twice;
عُقْبَى ʿuqbā six times; عَاقِبَةٌ ʿāqibatun 32 times; مُعَقِّب muʿaqqib once and
مُعَقِّبَات muʿaqqibāt once.

يُعَقِّب yuʿaqqib [imperf. of v. II intrans.] to turn on one’s heels,
to return (28:31) وَلَّى مُدْبِرًا وَلَمْ يُعَقِّبْ he turned and ran away and
would not return.

عَاقَبَ ʿāqaba [v. III trans.] 1 to punish, to take reprisals, to
retaliate (16:126) وَإِنْ عَاقَبْتُمْ فَعَاقِبُوا بِمِثْلِ مَا عُوقِبْتُمْ بِهِ if you have to take
reprisals, do so in proportion to the injury you have been
subjected to 2 to have one’s turn, to gain war booty (in an
interpretation of verse 60:11) وَإِنْ فَاتَكُمْ شَيْءٌ مِنْ أَزْوَاجِكُمْ إِلَى الْكُفَّارِ
فَعَاقَبْتُمْ فَآتُوا الَّذِينَ ذَهَبَتْ أَزْوَاجُهُمْ مِثْلَ مَا أَنْفَقُوا if any of you have wives who leave
you for the disbelievers, should the community subsequently
acquire [booty] from them, give those whose wives have gone [to
the disbelievers] the like of what they had spent.

عُوقِبَ ʿūqiba [pass. v. III] to be punished, to be subjected to
reprisals, to be retaliated against (22:60) وَمَنْ عَاقَبَ بِمِثْلِ مَا عُوقِبَ بِهِ so
who retaliates in equal measure to the injury he had been
subjected to.

أَعْقَبَ ʾaʿqaba [v. IV doubly trans.] to cause to happen to

632

investigate; eagle; to turn back; to repair; result; to avenge
oneself; to punish; to interlace; obstacle; to detain. Of this root,
14 forms occur 80 times in the Qur’an: يُعَقِّب yuʿaqqib twice; عَاقَبَ

subjected to 2 to have one’s turn, to gain war booty (in an

you for the disbelievers, should the community subsequently
acquire [booty] from them, give those whose wives have gone [to

عُوقِبَ ʿūqiba [pass. v. III] to be punished, to be subjected to
reprisals, to be retaliated against (22:60) وَمَنْ عَاقَبَ بِمِثْلِ مَا عُوقِبَ بِهِ so
who retaliates in equal measure to the injury he had been
subjected to.

أَعْقَبَ ʾaʿqaba [v. IV doubly trans.] to cause to happen to

632

entries.definition:
someone as a consequence or a result, to reward someone with, to
pay back (9:77) فَأَعْقَبَهُمْ نِفَاقًا فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ so as a consequence He made
hypocrisy settle in their hearts.

عُقْب ʿuqb [v. n.] outcome, result (18:44) هُوَ خَيْرٌ ثَوَابًا وَخَيْرٌ عُقْبًا He
is the best [to give a] reward and the best [to determine an]
outcome.

عَقِب ʿaqib [n., dual عَقِبَيْن ʿaqibayn; pl. أَعْقَاب ʾaʿqāb] heel,
posterity, descendants (43:28) وَجَعَلَهَا كَلِمَةً بَاقِيَةً فِي عَقِبِهِ and he made
it a word enduring among his descendants; *(2:143) يَنْقَلِبُ عَلَى عَقِبَيْهِ
he turns on his heels, retreats, goes back on his word; *(6:71) وَنُرَدُّ
عَلَى أَعْقَابِنَا and we are returned upon our heels, retracing our steps
[into disbelief].

عِقَاب ʿiqāb [v. n./n.] retribution, punishment (40:22) إِنَّهُ قَوِيٌّ شَدِيدُ
الْعِقَابِ He is indeed full of strength, severe in punishment.

عَقَبَةٌ ʿaqabatun [n.] obstacle, difficult uphill track, steep
mountain track (90:11) فَلَا اقْتَحَمَ الْعَقَبَةَ will he not brave the steep
path (or, difficult task)?

عُقْبَى ʿuqbā [v. n./n.] 1 ultimate result, outcome, sequel (13:22)
أُولَئِكَ لَهُمْ عُقْبَى الدَّارِ for those there will be the outcome of the final
abode [Paradise] 2 reward (13:35) تِلْكَ عُقْبَى الَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا وَعُقْبَى الْكَافِرِينَ
النَّارُ this is the reward that awaits those who are mindful of God;
the disbelievers’ reward is the Fire.

عَاقِبَةٌ ʿāqibatun [v. n./n.] 1 the end, consequence, the outcome
(47:10) كَيْفَ كَانَ عَاقِبَةُ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ how was the end for those before
them 2 reward (7:128) وَالْعَاقِبَةُ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ and the [good] reward belongs
to those who are mindful of Him.

مُعَقِّب muʿaqqib [act. part.] one whose job it is to reject or allow
a decision made by another (13:41) وَاللَّهُ يَحْكُمُ لَا مُعَقِّبَ لِحُكْمِهِ God
decides—no one can sanction or alter His decision.

مُعَقِّبَات muʿaqqibāt [pl. of fem. act. part. مُعَقِّبَة muʿaqqibatun]
attendants perpetually acting in succession one after the other
(13:11) لَهُ مُعَقِّبَاتٌ مِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِ يَحْفَظُونَهُ مِنْ أَمْرِ اللَّهِ
for him are attendants [angels ranged], before him and behind him, watching
over him by God’s command.

633

633
